## Changes

### Unified error taxonomy — [#196](https://github.com/velocitycareerlabs/WalletAndroid/pull/196), [#195](https://github.com/velocitycareerlabs/WalletAndroid/pull/195), [#192](https://github.com/velocitycareerlabs/WalletAndroid/pull/192), [#198](https://github.com/velocitycareerlabs/WalletAndroid/pull/198)

- **Error taxonomy**: introduce a unified `VCLErrorCode` taxonomy that classifies failures by validation phase — link/connectivity, client request authorization, issuer/verifier DID resolution, registration checks, and issuer/verifier request validity and authorization — replacing the previous platform-specific codes, with a general `sdk_error` fallback.
- **VCLError diagnostics**: add request and validation context to `VCLError` (`sourceErrorCode`, `validationPhase`, `requestDid`, `requestUri`, `requestKind`) plus native cause and stack-frame diagnostics, and add `errorCodeCompatibilityMode` (`Taxonomy` default, `Legacy` fallback) on `VCLInitializationDescriptor`.

## Backward incompatibilities

- By default the SDK now returns the new taxonomy codes and populates validation-phase and request context on `VCLError`. Set `errorCodeCompatibilityMode = VCLErrorCodeCompatibilityMode.Legacy` to keep the previous codes.
- Empty, missing, or malformed request JWTs are now classified as `issuer_request_invalid` / `verifier_request_invalid` during request validation, instead of surfacing later as DID resolution or public-JWK lookup failures.
